name: Alt del Pi and Garbí Loop from Estivella
name_original: Parc Natural de la Serra Calderona — Ruta circular al Garbí desde Estivella
description: Conquer the challenging Alt del Pi and Garbí Loop from Estivella, a difficult touring cycling route with rewarding views in Sierra Calderona
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Estivella are set within the Sierra Calderona mountain range in Valencia, Spain, offering diverse landscapes for cyclists. The terrain features a mix of mostly paved surfaces and challenging unpaved segments, with significant elevation gains across its rugged mountains. This area is characterized by abundant plant life and proximity to the Mediterranean Sea, contributing to a balanced climate suitable for outdoor pursuits. Best touring cycling routes around Estivella The most popular touring cycling route is Alt del Pi and Garbí Loop from Estivella, a 13.9 miles (22.4 km) trail that takes 1 hour 48 minutes to complete. This difficult route offers rewarding views within the Sierra Calderona Natural Park. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Ojos Negros Greenway – Ojos Negros Greenway loop from Algimia-Ciudad, a moderate 25.6 miles (41.2 km) path. This route allows cyclists to experience a segment of Spain's longest greenway, traversing varied landscapes. Local touring cyclists also love the Ferrolan Pool – Font del Comte loop from Algimia-Ciudad, a 18.4 miles (29.7 km) trail leading through challenging mountain terrain, often completed in about 2 hours 46 minutes. Touring cycling around Estivella is defined by the spectacular Sierra Calderona Natural Park, rugged mountains, and diverse vegetation. The network offers options for various ability levels, from moderate rides on greenways to difficult routes with significant elevation gains. The routes in Estivella are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 200 reviews. More than 3000 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Estivella's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Estivella?
A: Estivella offers a wide array of touring cycling routes, with over 150 options to explore in and around the area. These routes cater to various fitness levels, from moderate rides to challenging trails with significant elevation gains.

Q: What are the general difficulty levels for touring cycling in Estivella?
A: The touring cycling routes around Estivella offer a range of difficulties. You'll find approximately 7 easy routes, 54 moderate routes, and 89 difficult routes. This variety ensures options for all skill levels, from leisurely rides to demanding climbs within the Sierra Calderona.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes in Estivella?
A: The terrain around Estivella is diverse, featuring a mix of mostly paved surfaces suitable for all skill levels, alongside more challenging unpaved segments. You can expect significant elevation gains as many routes traverse the rugged mountains of the Sierra Calderona Natural Park.

Q: Are there any long-distance touring cycling options near Estivella?
A: Yes, Estivella provides access to longer touring cycling experiences. A notable option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38277000" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ojos Negros Greenway&lt;/a&gt;, which is Spain's longest greenway. You can access segments of it via loops like the one from Algimia-Ciudad, allowing you to experience varied landscapes across multiple provinces. Additionally, multi-day routes like the 'Pedaleando el Mediterraneo' combine nature, culture, and gastronomy.

Q: Can I find circular touring cycling routes in the area?
A: Yes, many touring cycling routes around Estivella are designed as loops, offering convenient starting and ending points. An example is the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e918738545/alt-del-pi-and-garbi-loop-from-estivella" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alt del Pi and Garbí Loop from Estivella&lt;/a&gt;, which provides rewarding views within the Sierra Calderona Natural Park.

Q: Are there family-friendly touring cycling routes in Estivella?
A: While many routes involve significant elevation, there are family-friendly options, particularly on the greenways. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31577614" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Footbridge – Rest area on the greenway loop from Estivella - Albalat dels Tarongers&lt;/a&gt; offer a moderate ride, often on mostly paved surfaces, which can be more suitable for families.

Q: Are touring cycling routes around Estivella suitable for cycling with dogs?
A: Many natural trails and greenways around Estivella are generally dog-friendly, especially if your dog is accustomed to cycling alongside you or in a trailer. However, always ensure your dog is on a leash where required, and be mindful of other trail users and local wildlife, particularly within the Sierra Calderona Natural Park.

Q: Where can I park my car when starting a touring cycling route in Estivella?
A: Parking is generally available in and around Estivella village. For specific routes, look for designated parking areas near trailheads or in nearby towns like Algimia-Ciudad, which serve as common starting points for many loops, including those accessing the Ojos Negros Greenway.

Q: Is public transport available to access touring cycling routes in Estivella?
A: Estivella is located in the inland province of Valencia. While local bus services might connect to nearby towns, direct public transport access to specific trailheads can be limited. For cyclists, it's advisable to check local bus schedules or consider driving to a convenient starting point. Some train stations in the wider Valencia region may accommodate bikes, but specific routes would require checking individual train line policies.

Q: Do I need any special permits to cycle in the Sierra Calderona Natural Park?
A: Generally, no special permits are required for cycling on established trails within the Sierra Calderona Natural Park. However, it's always recommended to respect park regulations, stay on marked paths, and be aware of any specific restrictions that might apply to certain areas or during particular seasons, especially concerning protected flora and fauna.

Q: What natural landmarks or scenic viewpoints can I discover along the touring cycling routes?
A: The Sierra Calderona Natural Park offers numerous natural landmarks. You can cycle towards magnificent vantage points like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1054060" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Garbí Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;, which provides breathtaking panoramic vistas over the Mediterranean Sea and the Morvedre Valley. Other natural highlights include the beautiful &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2785413" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;PortaCeli Gorge&lt;/a&gt; and the natural water spring at Font del Barraix.

Q: Are there any historical sites or cultural attractions accessible by bike in Estivella?
A: Yes, Estivella and its surroundings are rich in history. Within the village, you can explore the Baroque church of Santos Juanes. Cycling routes might also lead you past historical attractions such as the ancient Els Arcs aqueduct, the ruins of Beselga castle (of Muslim origin), and the Estivella bridge, offering a blend of natural beauty and cultural heritage.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy the most about touring cycling in Estivella?
A: The touring cycling routes in Estivella are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 200 reviews. Cyclists often praise the diverse landscapes, the rewarding views from vantage points like El Garbí, and the variety of routes catering to different skill levels within the spectacular Sierra Calderona Natural Park.
tips: Typical mountain of the region, the Garbí, has several different climbs and can be accessed by road, road, path or even climbing.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start the Alt del Pi and Garbí Loop from Estivella?
faqs.answer: The loop starts and ends in Estivella. While specific parking lots aren't detailed, you can typically find parking within the town of Estivella, which serves as a common starting point for routes into the Sierra Calderona Natural Park. Look for available street parking or designated areas in the town center.

faqs.question: Is the Alt del Pi and Garbí Loop from Estivella dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: This route is designed for touring bicycles, and while dogs are generally allowed in the Sierra Calderona Natural Park, bringing a dog on a challenging cycling route like this would be highly impractical and potentially unsafe for both the cyclist and the dog. It's best to leave your canine companions at home for this particular adventure.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this loop?
faqs.answer: The Sierra Calderona Natural Park offers pleasant cycling conditions for much of the year. However, to avoid the intense summer heat and potential winter chill, spring (April-May) and autumn (September-October) are generally considered the best times. During these seasons, you'll experience more moderate temperatures, making the demanding climbs more enjoyable.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for cycling in the Sierra Calderona Natural Park?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to cycle the Alt del Pi and Garbí Loop within the Parc Natural de la Serra Calderona. However, always respect park regulations, stay on marked paths, and practice leave-no-trace principles to preserve the natural environment.

faqs.question: What are the road conditions like on this touring bicycle route?
faqs.answer: While the loop primarily consists of paved surfaces, touring cyclists should be prepared for significant variations in road quality. The initial 500 meters of the climb to El Garbí from Estivella are in quite poor condition, with numerous potholes and loose gravel. Similarly, the descent from Garbí via 'la Frontera' is very poor, lacking guardrails and featuring holes, gravel, and inlaid concrete sections. Some advise against using this descent. These rough patches demand careful handling and robust tires, especially if you're carrying a heavier load.

faqs.question: How difficult is the Alt del Pi and Garbí Loop for touring cyclists?
faqs.answer: This loop is considered a difficult and challenging ride, requiring very good fitness. Both Alt del Pi and El Garbí feature significant climbs with consistently steep gradients, often well into double figures. The Alt del Pi climb from Estivella is about 7.2 km with an average gradient of 8.8%, while Port del Garbí is about 5.1 km with an average gradient of 9.2%. The combination of these steep ascents and potentially poor road surfaces makes it a demanding route, even for intermediate cyclists.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route primarily follows paved roads, but as a touring cyclist, you should anticipate sections where the pavement quality deteriorates significantly, featuring potholes, loose gravel, and even inlaid concrete. Some segments may be unpaved and difficult to ride, potentially requiring you to push your bike. The mountainous landscape means you'll encounter demanding ascents and descents throughout.

faqs.question: What are the main scenic highlights or viewpoints along the route?
faqs.answer: The loop offers very pleasant scenery with sweeping views across the Mediterranean forests of the Sierra Calderona Natural Park. From open sections of the climbs, you'll enjoy fine vistas. The panoramic views from the summit of El Garbí are particularly renowned and make the arduous climb worthwhile. Alt del Pi also provides beautiful views from its summit. Be sure to stop at the Garbí Viewpoint for stunning photo opportunities.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route intersects with several other paths. Notably, sections of the loop overlap with official trails such as [CIMA VA02] Alt del Pi * Torres Torres, [CIMA VA10] Alto del Garbí * Estivella, and Estivella - Penya Roja. You may also encounter parts of the PR-CV 369 and the historic Senda Vella al Garbí.

faqs.question: Is this a good route for beginner touring cyclists?
faqs.answer: No, this route is generally not recommended for beginner touring cyclists. It is classified as 'difficult' due to its demanding climbs with steep gradients and sections of poor road quality. It requires very good fitness, appropriate gearing, and sturdy equipment. Intermediate to advanced touring cyclists will find it more suitable.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or places to refuel near the route in Estivella?
faqs.answer: Estivella is a small town and a common starting point for cyclists. You can typically find local cafes or bars in the town center where you can refuel before or after your ride. It's always a good idea to carry sufficient water and snacks for the duration of the challenging loop, as services may be limited once you are on the climbs.
